author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

title: Chapter 68

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "‘Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: When will you see God, who are possessed with blindness in the impure malice of your hearts?"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: Wherefore say you this?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Can it be that we reproach all nations with the dark and hidden things which are declared by men, and do not choose to understand the manifest sayings which God spake in olden time of all the nations of the earth?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: This is indeed great blindness of heart; and if you do not recognize it in yourselves, that is even greater blindness.
